Arc drawing gesture.question: How should a curved (arc) edge be drawn?
Arc drawing gesture.answer: 3-click arc: start / on-arc / end. Chosen via interactive demo (spike 003c). Maps 1:1 to the validated 3-point solver and is the only gesture that can reach a major arc (>180deg).

Arc drawing gesture.question: Within one markup, how to make an edge an arc vs straight?
Arc drawing gesture.answer: Both: momentary hold-key (e.g. A) for a one-off arc edge + a sticky toggle for a run of arc edges. Default edge is straight.

Snap on/off & override.question: How should snapping be turned on/off during placement and editing?
Snap on/off & override.answer: Both: ON by default + momentary hold-key (e.g. Alt) to suspend for one point + persistent toggle (e.g. F3) to turn off for a section. Exact keys to be confirmed against Phase 9 bindings at plan time.

Snap on/off & override.question: What should the on-screen snap indicator look like?
Snap on/off & override.answer: Per-type glyphs (CAD-style): square = vertex/endpoint, triangle = nearest-point-on-segment; reserve X for intersection (later). Shape conveys snap type for accuracy trust.

Which snap targets ship.question: Which snap targets ship in this phase vs defer?
Which snap targets ship.answer: SHIP: vertex/endpoint snap, nearest-point-on-segment snap, close-the-loop snap (own start vertex). DEFER: intersection snap (reserve X glyph; spike 002 flagged O(k^2) cost), grid snap (out of scope unless requested). RULE: snap to other markups' geometry freely; for the in-progress/edited markup snap only to its own start vertex (never own intermediate vertex or the actively-dragged vertex). Snapping applies during both placement and editing (Phase 12 vertex/body drags).
options_presented: Ship vertex + on-segment + close-the-loop; defer intersection + grid (recommended)

deferred_ideas: How-To-Manual (end-user usage manual) — its own future phase; spans all features. Phase 14 must still emit manual-ready docs for its new tools/shortcuts/indicators.
Intersection snap — deferred to a later phase (spike 002: costliest target, needs separate validation). X glyph reserved now.
Grid snap — out of scope unless explicitly requested.
cross_cutting_constraints: Documentation deliverable: every new tool/gesture/shortcut/indicator added this phase must be documented manual-ready (shortcuts table + usage notes + indicator legend) as it's built. See memory project-howto-manual.
